title: Medicinische Gedichte aus einer Stockholmer Handschrift
summary: CME00098 encodes Ferdinand Holthausen’s two newly collated X 90 poem editions and German afterword (Anglia 18 (1896), printed pp. 295–331), excluding the article’s title, manuscript introduction, and method on pp. 293–294. The selection contains two distinct anonymous Middle English works: DIMEV 2343, a receipt collection whose displaced continuation Holthausen reinserts into the first poem, and DIMEV 4171, a 965-line herbal. Holthausen directly transcribed Kungliga biblioteket X 90 and collated against George Stephens’s 1844 printing. Complete supporting editions, catalogues, and the collateral Takamiya MS 46 facsimile are cached; no complete public X 90 facsimile was found, and KB’s response about a supplied facsimile and reuse remains pending.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00098
entities.description: The repository representation pinned in the CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic text CME00098
entities.description: A manually keyed, proofread, basic-TEI electronic representation of Holthausen printed pp. 295–331: two poem editions, 1,462 raw verse-line elements, manuscript and print milestones, rubric headings, notes, and a German afterword.
notes: The XML root declares “enm ger”; this record normalizes German to ISO 639-3 deu.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 17866
entities.description: A collateral paired witness of both works, ordered herbal then receipts rather than X 90’s receipts then herbal.

physical_description.extent: The catalog describes one item and contents extending at least through f. 42; the paired collateral versions occupy ff. 5r–21v.
notes: The British Library record broadly describes medicinal virtues, prescriptions, charms, an earlier verse portion beginning at f. 3, and a Latin-English Nomina herbarum at f. 42.
DIMEV and eLALME associate this copy's scribal language with Lincolnshire. That witness-level localization is not a composition place for either abstract work.
The current catalog supplies no linked digitized content. This is a dated access finding, not proof that no preservation photography exists.

entities.label: Ferdinand Holthausen, Medicinische Gedichte aus einer Stockholmer Handschrift (1896)
entities.description: Holthausen’s newly collated edition of two distinct verse works in KB X 90. CME00098 begins with “Gereimte Heilkunde” on printed p. 295, continues with “Heilkräuterlehre” on pp. 307–330, and includes the German afterword on p. 331; it omits the article introduction on pp. 293–294.

bibliographic.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, “Medicinische Gedichte aus einer Stockholmer Handschrift,” Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 18 (Neue Folge 6) (1896): 293–331.
bibliographic.title: Medicinische Gedichte aus einer Stockholmer Handschrift

notes: The two German poem headings are Holthausen’s supplied labels, not verified manuscript titles.
The first close is dated Gothenburg, November 1895; the afterword is dated Gothenburg, February 1896.

entities.label: George Stephens, Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript (1844)
entities.description: The first verified printing of the X 90 versions, preserving the manuscript’s raw order: receipt collection, herbal, then displaced receipt continuation.

bibliographic.citation: George Stephens, 'Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, Preserved in the Royal Library at Stockholm,' Archaeologia 30 (1844): 349-418; separately issued, London: J. B. Nichols and Son, 1844.
bibliographic.title: Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, Preserved in the Royal Library at Stockholm

notes: The correct editor is George Stephens (1813–1895), not Thomas Stephens.
Stephens says he copied literally, substituted an easier y for two manuscript letter forms, italicized expanded contractions, and left corruptions uncorrected.

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons complete PDF of Stephens’s 1844 offprint
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 84-page PDF derivative of Internet Archive item b22009620

entities.label: Kungliga biblioteket X 90
entities.description: The direct witness for both poems: receipt collection on manuscript pp. 35–47 with a displaced continuation on pp. 78–80, and herbal on pp. 49–78.

date_statements.label: KB card / Stephens dating
date_statements.value: latter half of the fourteenth century

date_statements.label: Holthausen dating
date_statements.value: probably end of the fourteenth century

date_statements.label: MEG-C/LALME main-hand dating
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: Applies to the main hand containing both poems.

physical_description.support: Paper except for two parchment leaves
physical_description.extent: 108 leaves / manuscript pages 1–216; target poems on pp. 35–80
physical_description.format: Small quarto; approximately 30 lines per page; red rubrics
notes: Stephens gives the main hand as pp. 1–91 and 105–216; MEG-C gives pp. 1–91 and 104–216. Another hand occupies the intervening section.
Stephens reports defective leaves and an old leather binding on cords; the manuscript formerly belonged to Christian Ravius and bore number 28.
MEG-C localizes the main hand to Norfolk; this is scribal language, not composition place.
No indexed complete public facsimile was found on 2026-07-13; this is not proof that it has never been digitized.

entities.label: Beinecke Library Takamiya MS 46
entities.description: A collateral paired witness: herbal ff. 3r–14v and receipts ff. 14v–20v, sharing f. 14v.

holding.notes: The IIIF metadata's Call Number value is 'Takamiya MS 46 1,' while its preferred citation and current collection usage identify Takamiya MS 46; both forms are preserved.

physical_description.extent: 43 leaves; the paired verse works occupy ff. 3r-20v
physical_description.format: One hand; single columns of 33 lines; English bookhand
notes: The official record dates and locates production in England and describes Middle English and Latin herbal material, red initials, headings, and words, and a modern vellum-board binding.

Yale describes the two opening poems as showing traces of East Anglian dialect; DIMEV/eLALME associates the historical Takamiya copy with southern Lincolnshire. These are witness-level scribal-language claims, not work composition places.

entities.label: Yale Digital Collections complete facsimile of Takamiya MS 46
entities.description: The complete 114-canvas, full-color Yale sequence, including covers, pastedowns, endleaves, every manuscript leaf, and the laid-in parchment fragment.

entities.label: DIMEV records 2343 and 4171
entities.description: DIMEV v1.0 records the receipt collection and herbal as distinct works, supplies IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, forms and subjects, and enumerates their wider witness traditions.
notes: Record 2343 lists six witnesses; record 4171 lists eighteen active witnesses plus historical ghost records.
Record 4171 cites Stephens 1844 pp. 394–395 for X 90, but direct inspection places the herbal on pp. 364–393 and the displaced receipt continuation on pp. 393–395.

entities.label: KB microfilm negative MfH 428
entities.description: Catalogued microfilm negative of X 90; exact coverage remains unverified.

entities.label: George Stephens, catalogue of British and French manuscripts at the Royal Library in Stockholm (1847)
entities.description: A manuscript catalogue, not a second text edition. Printed pp. 40–43 describe Medical Miscellanies XIV / X 90 and summarize the 1844 publication.

bibliographic.citation: George Stephens, Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm (Stockholm: P. A. Norstedt & Söner, 1847).
bibliographic.title: Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm

entities.label: Bavarian State Library complete facsimile of Stephens’s 1847 catalogue
MEG-C source catalogue entry for X 90, main hand
entities.description: Modern linguistic-manuscript catalogue entry for LALME profile 4665a, dating the main hand to the first quarter of the fifteenth century and localizing it to Norfolk.

object.description: Two poem editions and afterword, excluding article introduction
Receipt main run and displaced continuation
Stephens’s prior printing of the same X 90 poems

subject.description: Both poems in raw X 90 order

object.description: Both X 90 poems and displaced receipt continuation

scope.notes: The catalogue supports description, not the edited text.

object.description: X 90 main hand containing both poems

editorial_practices.source_feature: X 90's error-prone text and Stephens's 1844 printing
editorial_practices.representation: An emended reading text with manuscript readings in notes
editorial_practices.description: Holthausen reports collating Stephens against X 90, correcting scribal and editorial errors, restoring rhythm and rhyme, and recording manuscript readings beneath the text.

Holthausen says he expands abbreviations in italics, introduces modern punctuation and capitalization, places his supplements and Stephens's lineation in square brackets, retains superscript additions in round brackets, and prints manuscript red ink in bold.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Holthausen's italic expansions and superscript typography
editorial_practices.representation: 2,287 HI elements with REND='italic' and three SUP elements
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves typographic distinctions from Holthausen's print; it does not recover the exact manuscript abbreviation mark represented by each expansion.
CME00098 begins at printed p. 295, omitting article title, introduction, and method on pp. 293–294, while retaining both complete poem editions and the p. 331 afterword.
editorial_practices.scope: CME00098 relative to complete Holthausen article

local_copies.label: Complete 114-canvas Yale facsimile of Takamiya MS 46

work_portion.label: Collateral versions of both CME00098 works
notes: The two works share f. 14v / canvas 34. The herbal closes with 'This medicine is proved well a plight'; the red rubric 'For the hede warke' and incipit 'If a man or woman more or lesse' begin the receipt collection on the same surface.
The opening red rubric 'Hic incipiunt virtutes herbarum' and first herbal lines appear on f. 3r / canvas 11; the receipt collection ends on f. 20v / canvas 46.

Complete for all provider-exposed surfaces: covers, pastedowns, front and rear endleaves, ff. 1r-43v, and the laid-in parchment fragment recto and verso. Yale exposes no separately labeled spine or edge views.
The exact IIIF Presentation 3 manifest, 114 full-size images, and per-image URLs, sizes, and SHA-256 values are retained in the bundle inventory.
Representative images are full-color sRGB JPEGs around 2,200 x 3,760 pixels and visibly preserve parchment tone, red rubrics, initials, corrections, and binding features.

access.format: Full-color IIIF page images, auditable ZIP bundle, and generated PDF

access.format: Versioned XML data release in ZIP

rights.component: Underlying anonymous medieval herbal and medical-receipt texts
rights.basis: The texts are attested in a fourteenth- or fifteenth-century manuscript and far predate U.S. copyright terms.

rights.component: 1896 edition, transcription, emendations, and editorial matter
rights.basis: Published in 1896, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Any transcription choices, emendations, notes, or other editorial contribution satisfying the applicable originality threshold
rights.basis: Holthausen died in 1956. Swedish Copyright Act section 43 gives the ordinary term through the end of the seventieth year after the author's death, yielding a cautious end date of 2026-12-31 for protectable contributions.
notes: This is a term calculation, not a conclusion that every diplomatic transcription detail is sufficiently original to be protected; re-review on or after 2027-01-01.

rights.basis: Published in 1844, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: University of Michigan digital text and collection item
rights.basis: The item and Corpus of Middle English collection apply the RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States statement.
notes: The assertion is jurisdiction-specific and is not restated as a worldwide dedication.

rights.component: Yale manuscript photographs, IIIF derivatives, generated PDF, and item presentation
rights.basis: Yale's public-domain policy says Yale claims no rights in public-domain works and Beinecke welcomes public-domain use, while the exact IIIF item gives a general user-liability warning rather than an item license.
notes: Yale requires or requests an appropriate Beinecke citation and places responsibility for legal assessment on the user.
The manifest's exact Rights field is a caution, not a Creative Commons license. Preserve citation and link-back data with any reuse.
The local bundle remains Git-ignored; local caching records research access and does not itself authorize repository redistribution.

rights.component: Any new digital photographs or scans supplied by KB
rights.jurisdiction: Sweden and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No item-specific image delivery, license, or written reuse determination is yet recorded. KB's general page says it charges no usage or publication fee, but that is not an item-specific rights grant.
notes: The project's external enquiry is pending. Preserve KB's answer and delivery terms before Git redistribution or print-on-demand publication.

rights.basis: The versioned release includes a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license.
The exact Commons file page applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark.
Published in 1847, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff.
The exact IIIF manifest states No Copyright–Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0.
notes: Not an open license; do not redistribute in a commercial workflow without resolving provider terms.

rights.basis: The exact card page applies the Public Domain Mark.
Public download verified; no item-level open license was found in the consulted PDF.

evidence.summary: Verifies repository identity, selected article boundary, two poem divisions and afterword, and counts: 2 DIV1, 2 DIV2, 89 DIV3, 93 HEAD, 1,462 L, 2 LG, 631 MILESTONE, 356 NOTE, 2 P, 37 PB, and 2,287 italic HI.

evidence.summary: Supports stable item identity, public digital text, and Holthausen source attribution.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection and About page.
evidence.summary: The collection describes manual keying from public-domain editions, in-house proofreading and editing, basic TEI markup, bulk XML availability, public access, and the No Copyright - United States statement.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 17866, record 032-002028327.
evidence.locator: Title, scope and content, material, date, Digitised Content, and request sections
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, medicinal virtues and prescriptions, earlier verse at f. 3, Nomina herbarum at f. 42, vellum, fourteenth-century catalog date, empty digitized-content field, and physical-item request route.

evidence.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, 'Medicinische Gedichte aus einer Stockholmer Handschrift,' Anglia 18 (1896), 293-331, Internet Archive item angliazeitschrif18tbuoft.
evidence.locator: article pp. 293–331 / PDF 307–345; CME selection pp. 295–331 / PDF 309–345

evidence.citation: George Stephens, Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, 1844 separate offprint, Internet Archive b22009620.
evidence.locator: receipt pp. 349–363 and 393–395; herbal pp. 364–393; description pp. 416–418
evidence.summary: Verifies George Stephens’s identity, title, literal-copy policy, X 90 description, raw poem sequence, exact loci and line ranges, and Commons Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: Kungliga biblioteket, manuscript nominal catalog card 21655, X 90.
evidence.summary: Identifies X 90, older latter-half-fourteenth-century date, MfH 428 microfilm negative, and card-image Public Domain Mark.

notes: Negative search evidence is not proof that no digitization or non-public preservation image exists.

evidence.citation: National Library of Sweden, 'Order copies.'
evidence.locator: Order form, fees, and contact details
evidence.summary: Provides form, contact, foreign-order fees, formats, and no-use-fee statement, but no item-specific redistribution grant.

evidence.citation: National Library of Sweden, 'Find and request manuscripts and personal archives.'
evidence.locator: Search, Special Reading Room, and reproduction routes
evidence.summary: Explains manuscript catalog services, registered-reader onsite access, possible referral to a digital copy, facsimile, or microfilm, and the mediated copy service.

evidence.citation: Yale Library Digital Collections, 'Herbal in prose and verse,' object 16156709, Takamiya MS 46.
evidence.summary: Confirms title, ca. 1400-1425 English production, parchment, one hand, 43 leaves, 225 x 120 mm, Middle English and Latin, complete digitization, contents, provenance, citation, rights warning, and complete canvas inventory.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of Yale Takamiya MS 46 IIIF canvases 11, 34, and 46 within the complete cached sequence.
evidence.locator: f. 3r / canvas 11; f. 14v / canvas 34; f. 20v / canvas 46
evidence.summary: Verifies the herbal opening rubric and incipit, the shared f. 14v boundary where the herbal closes and red 'For the hede warke' rubric and receipt incipit begin, the receipt ending on f. 20v, red rubrication, one-column layout, and full-color image character.

evidence.citation: Yale Library, 'Reuse, Permissions, and Copyright Policies.'
evidence.summary: Places responsibility for legal assessment on users and identifies Yale's policy on open access to digital representations of works in the public domain.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, 'Copyright Questions.'
evidence.locator: Public Domain and Fair Use; citation requirements
evidence.summary: States that Beinecke usually does not own copyright, welcomes use of public-domain materials, places status and permission assessment on users, and requires citation of the Beinecke source and applicable rights information.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 2343 and 4171; manuscript entries BLAdd17866, StockhX90, and TokyoTak46; bibliography entries Holthausen1896 and Stephens1844.
evidence.summary: Supports two distinct works, IDs 2343/4171 and IMEV 1408/2627, title/incipit forms, couplet form, subjects, X 90 loci, wider witness census, collateral paired witnesses, bibliography, and CC BY 4.0.
notes: Its Stephens pp. 394–395 locator for the X 90 herbal conflicts with direct scan evidence placing the herbal on pp. 364–393.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Biographie, 'Holthausen, Ferdinand.'
evidence.summary: Gives Holthausen's life dates as 9 September 1860 to 19 September 1956 and GND 116969520.

evidence.citation: Sweden, Lag (1960:729) om upphovsrätt till litterära och konstnärliga verk, section 43.
evidence.summary: States the ordinary term through the end of the seventieth year after the author's death.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Works first published before the current statutory cutoff
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office term chart supports the public-domain status in the United States of works published in 1844, 1847, and 1896 as of 2026.

evidence.citation: CME00098 ignored source-cache integrity and hard-link audit.
evidence.summary: Verified signatures, hashes, byte counts, PDF/ZIP integrity, complete-source coverage, work mappings, and hard-link reuse for CME00098 cached sources.

evidence.citation: George Stephens, Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm (1847).
evidence.locator: X 90 description printed pp. 40–43 / IIIF canvases 56–59
evidence.summary: Confirms that this is a manuscript catalogue, supplies X 90 physical and contents description and 1843/1844 publication history, and exposes BSB’s NoC-NC statement.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Middle English Grammar Corpus Sources, version 2011.1, X 90 main-hand entry.
evidence.locator: catalogue p. 116 / PDF p. 117; LALME LP 4665a
evidence.summary: Dates the main hand to the first quarter of the fifteenth century, gives MED ?before 1450, identifies Cursiva anglicana formata, and localizes it to Norfolk.

evidence.citation: Sweden, Copyright Act (1960:729), section 49 a.
evidence.summary: Establishes a related right for qualifying photographic images; item-specific application to future KB images is unresolved.

open_questions.question: Does KB hold or supply a complete color digital facsimile of X 90, including all binding and manuscript surfaces, and what would it cost?
next_steps: Retain KB’s written response and quotation.
If supplied, cache the complete object and separately map pp. 35–47, 49–78, and 78–80.

open_questions.question: What item-specific terms govern Git redistribution and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand reuse of a newly supplied X 90 facsimile?
next_steps: Preserve written permission or delivery license.
Keep delivered images Git-ignored until terms are resolved.

open_questions.question: When and where were the two medieval works composed, and how do the X 90, Add MS 17866, Takamiya MS 46, and wider DIMEV versions relate?
next_steps: Review modern critical scholarship on DIMEV 2343 and 4171 and the Macer tradition.
Do not turn witness dates or dialect regions into composition claims.

open_questions.question: Should DIMEV 4171’s X 90 Stephens locator be corrected from pp. 394–395 to the directly verified herbal run on pp. 364–393?
next_steps: Report the discrepancy to DIMEV with scan evidence.
Use direct scan mapping while preserving the conflict.

open_questions.question: What exact X 90 abbreviation signs underlie the 2,287 italic spans inherited from Holthausen’s expanded print transcription?
next_steps: Collate a complete color facsimile against print and XML.
Do not infer manuscript signs solely from italics.

review_notes: Catalog boundary: Holthausen printed pp. 295–331 as encoded, not the complete article, either physical codex, or one homogeneous medieval treatise.

Primary path: XML artifact ← U-M encoding ← Holthausen selection ← X 90; Stephens 1844 is the collation source, Stephens 1847 descriptive support, and Add MS 17866/Takamiya MS 46 collateral witnesses.
